title: rosa pumila var. tricolor
intro: Rosa pumila var. tricolor, also known as the Dwarf Wild Rose or Tricolor Dwarf Rose, is a compact, thorny shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rosa pumila var. tricolor include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting healthy digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
